zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Luttrell III於1276年出生在英國林可爾史爾英厄姆時，他的父親Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham21歲，母親Joan de Taillebois20歲。他與Agnes Sutton結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1345年5月23日在他的家鄉享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾英厄姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Luttrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): from a diminutive of Old French loutre ‘otter’ (from Latin lutra), applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble an otter, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who hunted otters (for their pelts). Compare Luter and Litteral .

variants.name: Luter
meanings: from Old French loutre ‘otter’, either used as a nickname, or alternatively as a metonymic occupational name to denote an otter hunter.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Luttrell III was born in 1276, in Ingham, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham, was 21 and his mother, Joan de Taillebois, was 20. He married Agnes Sutton. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 23 May 1345, in his hometown, at the age of 69, and was buried in Ingham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Luttrell III est né en 1276, à Ingham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Joan de Taillebois, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Sutton. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 23 mai 1345, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Ingham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Luttrell III 1276 in Ingham, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham 21 und seine Mutter Joan de Taillebois 20. Er hat Agnes Sutton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Mai 1345 im Alter von 69 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Ingham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Luttrell IIIが1276年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアインガムで生まれた時，父Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Inghamは21歳，母Joan de Tailleboisは20歳であった。Agnes Suttonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1345年5月23日に故郷で69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアインガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Luttrell III(이)가 1276년 영국 링코실르 잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Taillebois(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Agnes Sutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1345년 5월 23일 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 잉함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Luttrell III nasceu em 1276, em Ingham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Taillebois, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Sutton. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de maio de 1345, em sua cidade natal, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Ingham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Luttrell III родился в 1276 г. в местности ингхам, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Joan de Taillebois, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Sutton. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 23 мая 1345 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности ингхам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Agnes Sutton. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 23 de mayo de 1345, en su pueblo, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Ingham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
